title: Iran Statistical Yearbook 1399 (2020/21) — Mining & Quarrying (Ch.6), Oil & Gas (Ch.7), Manufacturing Industries (Ch.8) chapters, retrieved via Wayback Machine
source_org: Statistical Centre of Iran (SCI / Markaz-e Amar-e Iran)
time_coverage: Iranian fiscal year 1399 (2020/21) edition; individual tables carry multi-year series back to 1380 SH (2001/02) at 5-year intervals plus 1394-1399 SH annually
frequency: annual (yearbook edition); table granularity varies (5-yearly intervals for older years, annual for most recent ~6 years)
units: described within each chapter PDF (cu m/day, 1000 cu m, mln cu m/day, billion cubic meters, etc.)
license_terms: SCI public publication; no explicit license stated on site
notes: Companion download to the already-held data/raw/sci-amar/sci-cpi-yearbook/ (chapters 4, 20, 21, 22, 23, 00) from a prior session, using the identical Wayback retrieval pattern (direct amar.org.ir TLS connection confirmed still reset/geo-blocked; not re-tested live, went straight to Wayback per documented prior finding). Downloaded specifically to check for physical-quantity production data (motor vehicles, cigarettes, refined sugar, processed tea, textile cloth, natural gas, oil products) per this session's task brief. RESULT: Chapter 7 (Oil & Gas) has genuinely useful physical-production/consumption tables (7.3 oil-product production, 7.4 oil-product consumption, 7.7 natural-gas production, 7.10 natural-gas consumers, 7.12 petrochemical production) sourced to Ministry of Oil / National Iranian Gas Company. Chapter 8 (Manufacturing Industries) does NOT have physical-quantity tables for specific consumer products (cigarettes/sugar/tea/textile) -- it is an ISIC-classified large-establishment survey (counts, employment, value-added, investment only); confirmed via full-text grep, zero hits for 'sugar', 'selected industrial products', 'physical production', 'quantity produced', 'passenger car', 'automobile'. Chapter 6 (Mining & Quarrying) downloaded but not deep-mined this session (out of the assigned cluster's core commodities; flagged for a future mining-focused pass).
